strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 funnels every payout failure through one PayoutError class and a single try/catch in main()'s payout case, while P1 scatters direct process.exit() calls across parseInputFlag, readPayoutInput, readPayoutRules, and runPayout, and even mixes styles by using console.error+process.exit(1) for a missing --input value versus JSON+process.exit(2) everywhere else.

P2's payout() call is wrapped in a try/catch with a payout_failed fallback that still emits a single JSON object to stderr with exit 2 for any unforeseen exception, and readPayoutRules further validates that fee/percent fields are well-typed numbers, whereas P1 has no such catch-all and no rules-field validation, so an unexpected error or a rules file missing a numeric field would leak a raw stack trace or silently produce NaN instead of the mandated single JSON error object.

P2's validateEvent emits distinct codes (missing_id, invalid_id, missing_merchant_id, unknown_event_type, invalid_amount_cents, missing_events, invalid_events) that mirror the spec's own enumerated list of failure categories, while P1's isValidEvent collapses every one of those spec-named categories into a single generic 'invalid_input' error.

P1's tests/cli.test.js defines two separate test() blocks both literally titled 'payout rejects conflicting duplicate ids' (a copy-paste artifact), while P2's four new tests each carry unique, descriptive names.
